title: Epic: TDD enforcement extension
description:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Phase is a property of the workspace, not a user-initiated mode. No /tdd start command.
- Two enforced phases (GREEN, RED) plus derived states RED_ARMED, TDD_VIOLATION, REGRESSION, idle.
- Phase derived from: git HEAD sha, `git status --porcelain=v2 --renames` hash, last authoritative `run_tests` tool result, per-branch baseline-failure set.
- HEAD change since stored state invalidates stored phase; fresh `run_tests` required before next guarded write.
- Clean tree on same HEAD keeps last known phase (commits do not flip phases).
- Failing-test counting uses vitest JSON report, strict scope: `{file::fullname}` ids in uncommitted files, minus per-branch baseline. 0 in-scope failures with diff → GREEN; 0 with clean tree → idle; 1 → RED; greater than 1 → TDD_VIOLATION. Compile or load error in an in-scope test file counts as 1. `it.skip`/`it.todo` ignored. `it.each` distinct per row.
- Failing tests outside uncommitted scope (and not in baseline) surface as REGRESSION, separate channel from RED count.
- Verification is exclusive to Tau's first-class `run_tests` tool. Phase state never updates from shell-observed test output.
- `run_tests` tool: typebox params `{scope: changed|file|all, path?, filter?}`. Binary resolved via `require.resolve('vitest/package.json')` from workspace root. Fixed args: `run --reporter=json --no-color`. Output capped (10 failures, 2KB/assertion, 32KB total). No `ctx.ui.confirm`.
- Runner result variants: `pass` | `fail` | `compile-error` | `no-tests-collected` | `timeout` | `runner-missing`. 30s timeout with process-group kill. JSON parse tolerates non-JSON preamble; parse failure → `fail`, never `pass`.
- Guard on file-write tools blocks wrong-phase edits using config globs after `path.resolve` → `fs.realpath` → repo-relative normalization; paths escaping repo rejected.
- Guard on bash tool (via `src/shell/` tree-sitter-bash) blocks: production writes in RED (including redirections, `tee`, heredocs, write-capable commands); test writes in GREEN; test-runner invocations (narrow list: `vitest`, `jest`, package-manager test subcommands, `node --test`, `npx`/`pnpx`/`bunx`/`dlx` test invocations); writes to `.tau/**`, `tau.config.json`, `.gitignore` in any phase.
- Path-class allowlist permits common non-TDD edits regardless of phase: `**/*.md`, `**/CHANGELOG*`, package.json version-bump diffs, comment-only diffs. All logged.
- Phase gate: transition to GREEN requires a `run_tests` record matching current statusHash.
- State persists to `.tau/state.json` via `src/workspaceState/` primitive. Atomic write (tmp+fsync+rename+fsync-dir). Advisory lock (`proper-lockfile` or native flock). Schema `version: 1`, unknown versions rejected, corrupt JSON quarantined.
- Config loaded from `tau.config.json` via `src/config/` primitive. Typebox-validated. Rejects empty `production` glob list, `**` at root in `production`, overlap between `tests` and `production`. Zero-config defaults work for TS+vitest repos.
- Bypass: `/tdd bypass <reason>` one-shot. Reason non-empty, not in stoplist (`fix`, `wip`, `bypass`, `.`, `tdd`, whitespace-only). Bound at issue to `(headSha, phase)`; rejected at consumption if either drifted. Consumed on next blocked tool call. Cleared on GREEN-producing run, HEAD change, or config change. No bypass for TDD_VIOLATION.
- Commands: `/tdd status`, `/tdd why`, `/tdd recheck`, `/tdd baseline`, `/tdd bypass <reason>`, `/tdd run-free <cmd>` (non-authoritative shell), `/tdd log` (ring buffer).
- Non-git workspaces fail closed (block all writes with actionable message). No silent degrade.
- Installer writes `.tau/` to `.gitignore` if missing.
- ADR-0005 (guard composition) lands before TDD guard code.
- ADR-0006 (workspace-state namespacing) lands before TDD state code.
- Commit extension refactored in same effort to consume `src/shell/` and `src/workspaceState/` primitives.
- Skill at `skills/tdd/SKILL.md`, Markdown-first (ADR-0004), phase-indexed, operational. Links `docs/policies/tdd-enforcement.md` as authoritative.
- Wire-format identifiers committed upfront (ADR-0003): `/tdd` command, `tdd.*` state/config namespace, `run_tests` tool name, `tdd-enforcement.md` policy slug, skill name `tdd`.

## Success criteria
- [ ] `src/workspaceState/` primitive with namespaced get/set/patch, atomic write, file lock, schema version check, quarantine-on-corrupt, unit tests
- [ ] `src/config/` primitive with typebox schema validation and zero-config defaults, unit tests
- [ ] `src/shell/` primitive lifted from commit extension; commit extension migrated to consume it; existing commit tests pass
- [ ] `src/extensions/tdd/runner/vitest.ts` produces the six typed result variants with hardened invocation
- [ ] `run_tests` tool registered with schema-validated params and capped output
- [ ] Phase derivation handles all states (GREEN, RED_ARMED, RED, TDD_VIOLATION, REGRESSION, idle) with HEAD-change invalidation and baseline subtraction
- [ ] Guard blocks all specified classes (file writes, bash writes, test invocations, self-protection) with path-class allowlist pass-through
- [ ] `/tdd status|why|recheck|baseline|bypass|run-free|log` commands functional
- [ ] Bypass one-shot, stoplist-validated, cleared on green/HEAD/config change, no bypass for TDD_VIOLATION
- [ ] Non-git workspace fails closed
- [ ] ADR-0005 and ADR-0006 merged
- [ ] `docs/policies/tdd-enforcement.md` authored with v1 limitations (vitest-only, bash-redirection gap history, AST tautology gap)
- [ ] `skills/tdd/SKILL.md` authored, phase-indexed
- [ ] Integration tests cover: RED→GREEN cycle, TDD_VIOLATION recovery, branch switch invalidation, bypass consumption, baseline snapshot
- [ ] All tests passing
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing

## Anti-patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO REFACTOR phase as guard-enforced state (reason: no enforceable predicate; turns into a vibe)
- NO explicit /tdd start or phase-transition commands (reason: contradicts always-on; phase is workspace-derived)
- NO runner primitive in v1 (reason: no second caller; `src/testRunner/` lifts when second adapter appears)
- NO guard/phase/bypass plugin registries (reason: speculative; no second caller)
- NO wall-clock TTL on bypass (reason: expired-but-unused is a second failure mode; semantic clearing suffices)
- NO commit-phase coupling (reason: orthogonal concerns)
- NO trust in model-reported or shell-observed test output (reason: violates "evidence over intention"; phase updates only from `run_tests` tool)
- NO `enabled: false` config flag (reason: enables trivial self-defeat; disable via uninstall)
- NO bash write-channel gap (reason: reviews flagged as correctness blocker; `src/shell/` is already available)
- NO silent non-git degradation (reason: fails polish bar; must fail closed with clear message)
- NO over-decomposed extension layout (reason: keep files few until a concept earns its own file)
- NO reason-length gate (reason: trivially defeated by padding; stoplist is effective)
- NO unauthenticated state trust (reason: always recompute from git + runner; state file is cache, not truth)
- NO bypass for TDD_VIOLATION (reason: normalizes the violation TDD exists to prevent; user must skip or delete)
- NO AST-diff test counting in v1 (reason: runner is authoritative substrate; tree-sitter-typescript is v2 scope)
- NO in-repo `@tau/runner` npm package (reason: friction without trust gain; install location is packaging, not security)
- NO silent command rewrite / process supervision / PATH injection / LD_PRELOAD (reason: violates "evidence over intention" and vision's out-of-scope "tools, tool calling")
- NO jest/mocha/pytest/go-test support in v1 (reason: one runner, pinned invocation, fewer variables)
- NO `vitest --related` transitive graph for RED scope (reason: conflates RED test with unrelated broken test; use strict uncommitted scope + REGRESSION channel)
- NO auto-run after every code-touching tool call (reason: phantom evidence and doubled runtime; phase gate forces `run_tests` structurally)

## Durable decisions
- Wire identifiers (ADR-0003 contracts): `/tdd` command prefix, state/config namespace `tdd.*`, tool name `run_tests`, policy slug `tdd-enforcement`, skill name `tdd`, subcommands `status|why|recheck|baseline|bypass|run-free|log`.
- State file path: `.tau/state.json`. Lock path: `.tau/state.lock`. Config file: `tau.config.json`. All v1.
- Test id scheme: `{file::fullname}` (describe chain + test name) — survives reformatting, breaks on rename (correct: renaming a failing test is semantic change).
- Runner invocation: `require.resolve('vitest/package.json')` from workspace root; args `run --reporter=json --no-color` plus scope-specific file list; no user-injectable args.
- JSON parse strategy: scan for first `{` at column 0 to skip preamble; parse failure → `fail`, never `pass`.
- Primitive boundary rule: code lives inside an extension until a second caller materializes.
- Commit extension migrates to consume `src/shell/` and `src/workspaceState/` in the same effort.
- ADR-0005 and ADR-0006 land before their consumers.

## Approach
Tau's TDD extension derives phase from git HEAD + working tree + the last authoritative `run_tests` tool invocation. A first-class Pi tool (`run_tests`) owns the vitest runner; phase state updates only from its result. A guard blocks wrong-phase edits (file-write tools) and wrong-phase shell writes (bash tool via `src/shell/` tree-sitter parse), plus a narrow ban on shell test-runner invocations that redirects to `run_tests`. Self-protection blocks writes to `.tau/**`, `tau.config.json`, and `.gitignore`.

Three shared primitives land first: `src/shell/` (lifted from `src/extensions/commit/shell/`), `src/workspaceState/` (new; `.tau/state.json` with atomic writes, flock, schema version, corruption quarantine, namespaced keys), and `src/config/` (new; `tau.config.json` with typebox schema). Commit extension is refactored to consume the new primitives so the patterns are proven across two extensions, honoring ADR-0001's "two callers → primitive" rule.

Two ADRs are prerequisites: ADR-0005 formalizes guard composition (deterministic ordering by priority, first-block-wins, `blockedBy` field) because TDD's guard coexists with commit's; ADR-0006 formalizes workspace-state namespacing so future extensions have a durable contract. The skill at `skills/tdd/SKILL.md` is the model's operational guide; `docs/policies/tdd-enforcement.md` is authoritative and documents v1 limitations (vitest-only, AST tautology gap, bash-redirection coverage scope). Non-git workspaces fail closed rather than silently degrading.

## Architecture
Layout:

```
src/
  config/
    index.ts
    schema.ts
    types.ts
    index.test.ts
  workspaceState/
    index.ts
    types.ts
    index.test.ts
  shell/
    index.ts
    types.ts
    index.test.ts
  extensions/
    commit/
      index.ts
      tool.ts
      guard.ts
      types.ts
    tdd/
      index.ts
      tool.ts
      guard.ts
      phase.ts
      paths.ts
      runner/
        index.ts
        vitest.ts
        types.ts
      types.ts
skills/
  tdd/
    SKILL.md
docs/
  adr/
    0005-guard-composition.md
    0006-workspace-state-namespacing.md
  policies/
    tdd-enforcement.md
```

Data flow: guard intercepts every tool call → consults phase.ts → phase.ts returns cached phase (keyed on headSha + statusHash + runId) or triggers re-derivation → guard blocks or allows per rules. `run_tests` tool writes new phase atomically through workspaceState primitive; cache invalidates on every blocking decision and every successful runner invocation.

## Design rationale

### Problem
Tau's vision (docs/foundations/vision.md) requires "workflow over suggestion, evidence over intention" — TDD discipline is the canonical instance. No existing extension enforces it. Without enforcement, models and humans drift into edit-then-test, multi-test specs, or shell-observed test results that bypass Tau's evidence channel.

### Research findings

**Codebase:**
- `src/extensions/commit/` (index, tool, guard, shell, types) establishes the extension shape — tool with typebox params + confirmation, bash guard via tree-sitter, skill delegation via `sendUserMessage`.
- `src/extensions/commit/shell/` — tree-sitter-bash integration. Promotes to `src/shell/` primitive with a second caller (TDD guard).
- ADR-0001 — primitives require a plausible second caller; extensions own feature-specific code.
- ADR-0003 — wire-format names are contracts; all TDD identifiers committed upfront.
- ADR-0004 — Markdown-first skills link to authoritative policies.
- `vendor/tree-sitter-bash.wasm` — existing wasm asset for shell parsing.

**External:**
- `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent` — `registerTool`, `registerCommand`, `on('tool_call') → {block, reason}`, `exec`, `ctx.ui.confirm`, `ctx.cwd`, `ctx.hasUI`, `sendUserMessage`, `defineTool` + typebox.
- Vitest `--reporter=json` emits per-test results with file paths and statuses; exit codes distinguish fail/config/compile errors.
- `proper-lockfile` (or native flock) for cross-session advisory locking.

### Approaches considered

#### 1. Fingerprint (selected)

**What:** Phase derived from git + last `run_tests` result; first-class tool owns runner; three shared primitives; narrow bash ban as signpost.
**Pros:** Honors always-on; runner-authoritative phase; pattern matches commit extension; primitives have immediate second callers (commit refactor).
**Cons:** Requires git; v1 vitest-only.
**Chosen because:** Only approach satisfying "always-on, no explicit start" with "evidence over intention"; primitive choices proven across two extensions in v1.

#### 2. Phase-as-file (rejected)

**What:** Two phases stored in a file; explicit `/tdd red`/`/tdd green` transitions.
**Why explored:** Minimal mechanism, zero derivation logic.
**Why rejected:** Explicit transitions contradict the locked "always-on, no start command" requirement.
**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Git-based derivation proves unreliable after real-world use.

#### 3. Phases as plugins (rejected)

**What:** Six new primitives (config, workspaceState, guards, phases, testRunner, bypass), registries for each, pluggable phase model.
**Why explored:** Speculative future-proofing for cape-style extension fleet.
**Why rejected:** No concurrent extension justifies the primitives; YAGNI per ADR-0001's "two callers" rule.
**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Two or more concurrent extensions need shared phase/guard/bypass plumbing.

#### 4. In-repo `@tau/runner` package (rejected)

**What:** Users install `@tau/runner` as dev dep; extension invokes that binary.
**Why explored:** Would isolate vitest resolution to user's project.
**Why rejected:** Per-repo install friction; install location is packaging, not security boundary. Phase-update-from-Tau-owned-invocation is the real trust boundary.
**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** User-local vitest plugin loading becomes a hard requirement.

#### 5. Process supervision / PATH injection / command rewrite (rejected)

**What:** Shim binaries on PATH, rewrite `pnpm test` to tau-run, LD_PRELOAD vitest.
**Why explored:** Would catch model's shell attempts transparently.
**Why rejected:** Violates "evidence over intention" (deceives model) and vision's out-of-scope "tools, tool calling". Cross-platform fragility.
**DO NOT REVISIT.**

#### 6. AST-diff test counting (deferred to v2)

**What:** Tree-sitter-typescript parses test-file diffs; count net-new `it()`/`test()` additions.
**Why explored:** Catches "tautological test" — model adds a test that passes immediately, no RED observed.
**Why rejected for v1:** Runner JSON is authoritative substrate; tree-sitter-typescript is new primitive with ~3MB wasm; known gap documented in policy doc.
**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Tautological-test detection becomes a blocker in practice.

### Scope boundaries

**In scope:** vitest-only runner, two-phase enforcement with RED_ARMED/TDD_VIOLATION/REGRESSION derived states, git-HEAD-bound state, baseline per branch, bash write guard + test-invocation ban, self-protection of `.tau/**`, one-shot bypass, seven commands, skill + policy doc, ADR-0005 and ADR-0006, commit extension refactor to consume new primitives.

**Out of scope:** REFACTOR enforcement (no predicate), non-vitest runners (separate epic), monorepo per-package phase, multi-stream concurrent phases, watch-mode handshake, structured bypass enums, commit-trailer requirements, HMAC state, rate-limited bypass, session kill-switch, AST-diff tautology detection, process supervision, in-repo runner package.

### Open questions
- Pi edit/write tool kind strings — enumerate by reading `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent` source during first implementation task.
- Path-class allowlist specifics (package.json version-bump diff-shape heuristic, comment-only diff detection) — define in execute-plan; heuristic fails closed on ambiguity.
- ADR-0005 scope — guard composition as internal utility vs shared module; lean internal utility until third caller.
- Schema migration trigger for `workspaceState` / `tau.config.json` — manual migration vs auto-upgrade on read; decide when v2 needed.
- `/tdd log` output format — pretty tabular vs JSONL; pagination.
- TDD_VIOLATION recovery — whether git-pathspec-revert operations are detected and allowed without bypass.
- Run-id semantics — statusHash alone vs process-metadata-derived id for phase-gate matching.

## Design discovery

### Key decisions made

| Question | Answer | Implication |
|---|---|---|
| TDD's role in Tau | One extension among many | Primitives namespaced and shared |
| Start model | Always-on, no command | Phase derived from workspace |
| RED/GREEN enforcement | Hard block wrong-class edits | Guard on edit/write + bash |
| Verification source | Tau's `run_tests` tool | Binary pinned, args locked, JSON parsed |
| Phase updates | Only from `run_tests` tool | Shell test invocations never update phase |
| Shell test invocations | Banned (narrow list) | Signpost, not the lock |
| Debug escape | `/tdd run-free <cmd>` | Non-authoritative output |
| Commit coupling | None | Phases orthogonal |
| Primitive name | `workspaceState` (kept, not `state`) | Disambiguates from extension-local state |
| Bypass shape | `/tdd bypass <reason>` one-shot, stoplist | Cleared on GREEN / HEAD / config change |
| REFACTOR | Folded into GREEN | Two phases |
| Phase source | Git + last runner result | Bound to HEAD sha |
| Branch switch | Stored phase invalidated | Forces re-derivation; bypass cleared |
| Runner location | Inside `extensions/tdd/runner/` | Lift to primitive when 2nd adapter appears |
| Runner coverage | Vitest only in v1 | jest/pytest/go deferred |
| Bash write guard | Yes, v1 | Closes channel gap |
| Self-protection | Block `.tau/**`, `tau.config.json`, `.gitignore` | Prevents trivial disable |
| Non-git | Fail closed | Polish > permissiveness |
| Docs/package.json | Path-class allowlist | Common edits don't need bypass |
| Baseline failures | Snapshot per branch | Pre-existing red bar doesn't pin RED |
| TDD_VIOLATION | Greater than 1 failing tests, no bypass | User must reduce to one |
| RED_ARMED | Test edited, run stale | Forces fresh `run_tests` |
| REGRESSION | Unrelated tests breaking | Separate channel |
| Counting | From vitest JSON, strict scope | Uncommitted files only |
| Test id | `{file::fullname}` | Survives reformatting |
| `it.skip`/`todo` | Ignored | Placeholders |
| `it.each` | Distinct per row | Forces discipline |
| AST-diff | Deferred v2 | Runner authoritative |
| Guard composition | ADR-0005 before TDD guard | Deterministic ordering, `blockedBy` field |
| Commit refactor | Same effort | Proves primitives across two extensions |

### Dead-end paths

#### HMAC-signed state file
**Why explored:** Security review flagged state tampering (model edits `.tau/state.json` to flip phase).
**What found:** Always-recompute from git + runner makes state a cache, not truth; tampered state overridden on next derivation.
**Why abandoned:** Authentication without a real integrity gain; adds key management burden.

#### Structured bypass reason enum plus commit-trailer requirement
**Why explored:** Harden bypass against abuse via structured categories and mandatory commit trailers.
**What found:** User wants simple UX; stoplist + log provides sufficient auditability.
**Why abandoned:** Too heavy for solo-dev tool.

#### Wall-clock bypass TTL
**Why explored:** Prevent "stale bypass silently applies later" foot-gun.
**What found:** Adds expired-but-unused failure mode; GREEN-change / HEAD-change / config-change clearing is sufficient and has no clock dependency.
**Why abandoned:** Second failure mode for marginal gain.

#### `vitest related` transitive graph for RED scope
**Why explored:** Initially seemed like correct "changed file" granularity.
**What found:** Transitive imports conflate "my RED test" with "unrelated broken test"; setup-file edits explode scope to full suite.
**Why abandoned:** Use strict uncommitted-files scope; add REGRESSION state for unrelated breakage.

#### Auto-run after every code-touching tool call
**Why explored:** Obvious way to keep phase current.
**What found:** Doubles runtime cost; creates phantom evidence; model forgets less than expected.
**Why abandoned:** Phase gate (refuse to advance to GREEN without matching `run_tests` record) structurally forces the call without spying on every edit.

### Open concerns raised
- Flaky tests polluting baseline → baseline is per-branch, snapshotted once, `/tdd baseline` refreshes; flaky-test handling deferred.
- Two Pi sessions on same workspace → advisory lock (flock / proper-lockfile) around every state read-modify-write.
- State corruption mid-write → atomic tmp+rename+fsync; quarantine on corrupt JSON.
- Monorepo / multi-package → deferred; single global phase documented as limitation.
- Bash-redirection coverage completeness → v1 covers redirections, heredocs, tee, and classified write-capable commands; `eval`/backticks with dynamic content blocked unless allowlisted.

title: Task 1: workspaceState primitive + ADR-0006
description:
## Goal
Deliver the `src/workspaceState/` primitive: a namespaced, atomically-written, file-locked JSON store at `.tau/state.json` with schema versioning and corruption quarantine. Ship with ADR-0006 documenting the namespacing contract. No TDD extension consumes it yet — this task is validated by its unit tests and its ADR.

Vertical slice: ADR + primitive implementation + schema + public API + unit tests. End-to-end deliverable that the TDD extension (and commit, in a later task) will consume verbatim.

## Execution mode
AFK — scope is fully specified, no user-facing decisions remain.

## Behaviors
Each becomes exactly one RED-GREEN-REFACTOR cycle.

1. Reading a fresh namespace returns an empty object when no state file exists
2. Writing under a namespace and reading it back returns the same value
3. Writing to one namespace does not affect another namespace's contents
4. An in-progress write never leaves `.tau/state.json` in a partially-written state (atomic via tmp+fsync+rename)
5. Corrupt JSON on disk is quarantined to `.tau/state.json.bak` and the load resets to empty state
6. A `version` field mismatching the current schema version rejects the load with a clear error
7. Concurrent read-modify-write sequences from two callers serialize via advisory lock (`.tau/state.lock`)
8. The first write creates `.tau/` directory if it does not already exist
9. Public API exposes `namespace(name).get()`, `namespace(name).set(value)`, `namespace(name).patch(partial)` with typebox-friendly types
10. Reading a namespace returns an independent copy (callers cannot mutate internal state)

## References
- `src/extensions/commit/tool.ts:110-187` — typebox + `ExtensionAPI` patterns to mirror for primitive API shape
- `src/extensions/commit/shell/index.ts` — primitive shape precedent (index.ts + types.ts + tests colocated)
- `docs/adr/0001-application-structure.md` — primitive boundary rules (`src/<primitive>/`, camelCase directory, `index.ts` with real implementation, no barrel files)
- `docs/adr/0002-file-naming-conventions.md` — `foo.test.ts` colocated; `types.ts` next to `index.ts`
- `docs/adr/TEMPLATE.md` — ADR structure to use for ADR-0006

## Deliverables
- `src/workspaceState/index.ts` — public API (`namespace()`, `get()`, `set()`, `patch()`)
- `src/workspaceState/types.ts` — exported types; typebox schemas for the envelope
- `src/workspaceState/index.test.ts` — unit tests covering the 10 behaviors above
- `docs/adr/0006-workspace-state-namespacing.md` — namespacing contract (per-extension top-level keys, schema version, migration policy, reserved namespaces `tdd`, `commit`), following `docs/adr/TEMPLATE.md` verbatim

## Success criteria
- [ ] ADR-0006 merged following `docs/adr/TEMPLATE.md` structure
- [ ] `src/workspaceState/` implements all 10 behaviors
- [ ] Atomic write verified: simulated crash mid-write leaves either old content or new content, never truncated
- [ ] Concurrent write test demonstrates lock serialization
- [ ] Corrupt JSON quarantine verified (bad JSON in file → quarantined to `.tau/state.json.bak`, load returns empty)
- [ ] Schema version mismatch rejected with informative error
- [ ] `pnpm test` passes
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks pass (oxlint, prettier, stylua-equivalent for TS)

## Out of scope
- Consuming the primitive from `tdd` or `commit` extensions (separate tasks)
- Schema migration tooling (v2 concern — note in ADR as open)
- File watcher for external edits (not required by any consumer today)
design:
## Refinement (task-refinement)

Applied via cape:task-refinement after codebase-investigator verification.

## Strengths confirmed
- 10 numbered behaviors are each testable and sequenced for RED-GREEN-REFACTOR.
- File references (`src/extensions/commit/tool.ts:110-187`, commit shell primitive, ADR template) resolve correctly.
- Primitive shape aligns with ADR-0001 and ADR-0002 (index.ts + types.ts + `foo.test.ts` colocated).

## Refinements applied

### Stale references
- Success criterion previously mentioned "stylua-equivalent for TS"; the repo uses `oxlint`, `oxfmt`, and `prettier` via `vite-plus` (pre-commit hooks at `.vite-hooks/_/pre-commit`). Replace with "oxlint, oxfmt, prettier".
- Test command is `pnpm test` which runs `vitest run --passWithNoTests` (vitest 4.1.4). Confirmed.

### Durable decisions locked (were vague or missing)
- **Locking:** adopt `proper-lockfile` as a dependency. Chosen over native `flock` because it handles stale-lock recovery (dead-process detection via mtime heuristic) portably across macOS/Linux. Task adds `pnpm add proper-lockfile` and `pnpm add -D @types/proper-lockfile` as a prerequisite step.
- **Envelope schema:**
  ```ts
  type Envelope = { version: 1; namespaces: Record<string, unknown> };
  ```
  Unknown `version` values, missing `version`, missing `namespaces` key, and non-object `namespaces` all count as "unknown version" for rejection purposes.
- **API shape:**
  ```ts
  interface WorkspaceState {
    namespace<T>(name: string): Namespace<T>;
  }
  interface Namespace<T> {
    get(): Promise<T | undefined>;
    set(value: T): Promise<void>;
    patch(partial: Partial<T>): Promise<void>;
  }
  ```
  Callers supply `T`; the primitive does not validate via typebox itself. Consumers (TDD, commit) validate their own namespace payloads at their boundary.
- **Copy semantics:** `get()` returns a deep copy via `structuredClone`. Mutating the returned object does not affect stored state.
- **Atomic write mechanism:** write to sibling tmp file `state.json.tmp.<pid>.<rand>` in same directory → `fsync(fileFd)` → `rename(tmp, final)` → open directory → `fsync(dirFd)` → close directory. Document platform note: directory fsync is a no-op on Windows; tests accept either behavior there.
- **Stale-lock policy:** `proper-lockfile` defaults (stale after 10s, update every 5s) are accepted as v1.

### Edge cases added (each maps to a test)
Appended to Behaviors list as 11-15:

11. Non-existent state file on first read returns an empty object for every namespace.
12. State file exists but `version` key is missing → load rejects with the same error as unknown version; original file quarantined to `.tau/state.json.bak`.
13. State file exists with valid JSON and correct `version` but `namespaces` is not a plain object → load rejects and quarantines.
14. Stale `.tau/state.lock` left by a dead process is recovered by `proper-lockfile`'s staleness check; next writer proceeds without manual intervention.
15. Mutation of a value returned by `get()` does not leak into subsequent reads (deep-copy verification).

### Failure modes specified
- `EACCES` on lock, tmp write, or rename → surface the underlying error as-is (no retry, no swallow). Tests cover this via a chmod-based setup.
- `ENOSPC` on tmp write → same surfacing policy. Test covered by a fake fs injection if native `ENOSPC` simulation is impractical; otherwise skipped with justification.
- Concurrent-write test (behavior 7) concretized: two callers run 100 interleaved `patch({count: prev+1})` cycles each; post-run total equals 200 and no writes are lost. Without the lock this demonstrably races.
- Crash-mid-write test (behavior 4) concretized: inject a spy on `fs.rename` that throws after one invocation; verify the existing file is unchanged (still contains prior value) and the tmp file is cleaned up on next successful write.

### Scope addition
- **Append `.tau/` to `.gitignore` as part of this task.** Rationale: without it, any subsequent task that triggers a write (even a test fixture) risks staging `.tau/state.json`. Low cost, prevents contamination. Also a listed requirement for the TDD extension; lifting it forward costs nothing.

### Implementation checklist additions
- `package.json` gains `proper-lockfile` runtime dep and `@types/proper-lockfile` dev dep.
- ADR-0006 explicitly documents: reserved top-level namespaces `tdd`, `commit`; version field semantics; migration policy (v1 manual, fail-loud on unknown version; v2 strategy deferred); platform note on directory fsync.

### Success criteria tightened
- Replace "oxlint, prettier, stylua-equivalent for TS" with "oxlint, oxfmt, prettier (enforced via vite-plus pre-commit hook at `.vite-hooks/_/pre-commit`)".
- Add: "`.tau/` present in `.gitignore` at task close".
- Add: "`proper-lockfile` added to `package.json` dependencies; `pnpm install` succeeds; no peer-dep warnings".
- Concretize "Concurrent write test" criterion to the 200-interleaved-patch measurable above.
- Concretize "Atomic write verified" criterion to the rename-spy injection described above.

title: Task 2: config primitive
description:
## Goal
Deliver the `src/config/` primitive: a `tau.config.json` loader with typebox validation and zero-config defaults tuned for TS+vitest repos. No extension consumes it yet — this task is validated by its unit tests. Later tasks wire it into `tdd` and `commit`.

Vertical slice: schema + defaults + loader + public API + unit tests. End-to-end deliverable that TDD guard/runner and commit will consume verbatim.

## Execution mode
AFK — scope is fully specified, no user-facing decisions remain.

## Behaviors
Each becomes exactly one RED-GREEN-REFACTOR cycle.

1. Missing `tau.config.json` returns defaults tuned for TS+vitest
2. Valid `tau.config.json` parses and merges over defaults
3. Invalid JSON surfaces a clear error (no silent fallback to defaults)
4. Empty `production` glob list is rejected with an actionable error naming the field
5. `**` at root in `production` is rejected with an actionable error naming the field
6. Overlap between `tests` and `production` globs is rejected with an actionable error naming both fields
7. Unknown top-level keys are rejected by the strict typebox schema
8. Relative globs are normalized against the repo root (loader input is `rootDir`)
9. Public API `loadConfig(rootDir?)` returns a deep copy; mutating it does not affect subsequent loads

## Defaults (locked)
- `production`: `['src/**/*.{ts,tsx}']`
- `tests`: `['**/*.test.ts', '**/*.spec.ts']`
- Semantic: `production` is interpreted as "after subtracting tests" by consumers; the loader does not subtract at parse time.

## References
- `src/workspaceState/index.ts` — primitive layout, dependency-override, deep-copy pattern to mirror
- `src/workspaceState/types.ts` — typebox envelope schema precedent
- `src/extensions/commit/tool.ts:110-187` — typebox parameter schema precedent
- `docs/adr/0001-application-structure.md` — primitive boundary rules (`src/<primitive>/`, `index.ts` with real implementation, no barrel files)
- `docs/adr/0002-file-naming-conventions.md` — `index.ts` + `types.ts` + `schema.ts` + `index.test.ts` colocated

## Deliverables
- `src/config/index.ts` — public `loadConfig(rootDir?)` API
- `src/config/schema.ts` — typebox schema + locked defaults + cross-field validators (empty-production, root-glob, overlap)
- `src/config/types.ts` — exported `Config` type derived via `Static<typeof configSchema>`
- `src/config/index.test.ts` — unit tests covering all 9 behaviors

## Success criteria
- [ ] All 9 behaviors unit-tested
- [ ] Zero-config path verified in a TS+vitest fixture (no `tau.config.json` present → defaults returned)
- [ ] Each of the three rejection rules (empty production, `**` root, tests/production overlap) surfaces an error that names the offending field(s)
- [ ] `pnpm check` passes (typecheck, oxlint, ast-grep, oxfmt, prettier, vitest)

## Out of scope
- ADR-0005 (guard composition) — ships with the TDD guard task
- Config versioning / migration — v2 concern; note as open in follow-up
- File watcher for live reload — no consumer today
- Consumption from `tdd` or `commit` extensions — later tasks
design:
## Refinement (task-refinement)

Applied via cape:task-refinement after codebase-investigator verification.

## Strengths confirmed
- 9 numbered behaviors are each testable and sequenced for RED-GREEN-REFACTOR.
- Locked defaults removed ambiguity up front.
- `src/workspaceState/` references confirmed: dependency-override pattern (line 229-231), `structuredClone` deep-copy (line 61, 271, 282, 293, 303), colocated `index.ts`+`types.ts`+`index.test.ts`.
- Typebox version confirmed: 0.34.49. `Type.Object`, `Type.Record`, `Type.Literal`, `Static<typeof>` all available and in use in workspaceState.

## Refinements applied

### Stale references
- `src/extensions/commit/tool.ts:110-187` is wrong; the typebox schema lives at **lines 26-31**. Lines 110-187 are the `execute` body. Replace reference with `src/extensions/commit/tool.ts:26-31`.
- ADR-0002 prescribes `foo.test.ts`; workspaceState uses `index.test.ts` in practice. Stay consistent with the workspaceState precedent (`index.test.ts`) so tests colocate with the single-file primitive.

### Durable decisions locked (were vague or missing)

- **`schema.ts` filename** is mandated by the epic's Architecture section (`src/config/index.ts | schema.ts | types.ts | index.test.ts`) even though ADR-0001/0002 do not explicitly endorse it. The epic is immutable — keep `schema.ts`. Split: `types.ts` holds the `Config` type alias (`Static<typeof configSchema>`); `schema.ts` holds the typebox schema, locked defaults, and cross-field validators. `index.ts` holds the loader.

- **Error shape:** introduce `ConfigValidationError extends Error` with public `field: string` (or `string[]` for cross-field) and `reason: string`. Every rejection throws this class; tests assert both `error.field` and `error.reason`.

- **"`**` at root in `production`" rule:** reject any pattern whose normalized form equals `**`, `**/*`, or `**/**`. Normalization = trim, collapse repeated slashes, strip leading `./`. Error: `{field: 'production', reason: 'root-glob **'}`.

- **"Overlap between `tests` and `production`" rule:** adopt a pragmatic heuristic, since general glob-set intersection is undecidable. Reject when either holds:
  1. The two arrays share any literal string element.
  2. Any `production` pattern ends in `.test.{ts,tsx}`, `.test.js`, `.spec.{ts,tsx}`, or `.spec.js`.
  Document the heuristic in the `schema.ts` validator; link from an inline comment. Algorithmic intersection deferred to v2.

- **Empty `tests` array is allowed** (repos without tests are valid); only empty `production` is rejected. Lock this.

- **Duplicate globs within a single list are allowed** — deduped silently by the loader after validation. No error, no warning.

- **Symlinked `tau.config.json`:** follow by default (node's `readFile` default). Not re-statted.

### Edge cases added (each maps to a test)
Appended to the Behaviors list as 10-14:

10. `tau.config.json` exists but is empty (zero bytes) → rejected as invalid JSON (same error path as behavior 3, not treated as "missing").
11. `tau.config.json` contains `{}` → valid; all fields fall back to defaults.
12. Non-string elements in `production` or `tests` arrays → rejected by typebox schema.
13. Duplicate entries within a single glob list → accepted, deduped silently.
14. `tau.config.json` is a directory (`EISDIR`) or unreadable (`EACCES`) → surface the underlying error as-is (no silent fallback to defaults, same policy as invalid JSON).

### Failure modes specified
- `ENOENT` on `tau.config.json` → return defaults. Only `ENOENT` triggers this path.
- `EACCES` / `EISDIR` → surface as-is (tested via behavior 14).
- Typebox validation errors and cross-field-validator errors → wrapped in `ConfigValidationError` with `field` and `reason`.
- Invalid JSON → wrapped in `ConfigValidationError` with `field: '<root>'`, `reason: 'invalid JSON: <parser message>'`.

### Scope additions
- None. Epic requirements are immutable; refinement only tightens.

### Success criteria tightened
- Replace "each rejection rule surfaces an error that names the offending field(s)" with: "each rejection throws `ConfigValidationError` with `field` and `reason` properties; tests assert both."
- Add: "ast-grep rule `types-before-runtime-code` passes on `src/config/index.ts` and `src/config/schema.ts` (types declared before runtime exports)."
- Add: "`ConfigValidationError` is exported from `src/config/index.ts` so consumers can `instanceof`-check it."

### Implementation checklist additions
- `src/config/schema.ts`: typebox `configSchema` with strict object (no additional properties), locked defaults, cross-field validators for empty-production / root-glob / overlap / path-normalization.
- `src/config/types.ts`: `Config` type alias via `Static<typeof configSchema>`; `ConfigValidationError` class declaration (types before runtime — verify ast-grep passes).
- `src/config/index.ts`: `loadConfig(rootDir?: string): Promise<Config>` — reads file, parses, merges with defaults, runs cross-field validators, returns deep copy via `structuredClone`.
- `src/config/index.test.ts`: mirror workspaceState test harness (`mkdtemp`, `tempDirs` cleanup, real filesystem, no mocking); one `describe('loadConfig')` with one `it` per behavior 1-14.
close_reason: Implemented src/config primitive with validation, defaults, loader API, and tests

title: Task 3: shell primitive + commit migration
description:
## Goal
Lift the bash AST parser out of `src/extensions/commit/shell/` into a new `src/shell/` primitive with two callers: (1) the TDD guard (later task) and (2) the migrated commit extension. Ship with the commit extension refactored to consume both `src/shell/` and `src/workspaceState/`, proving the primitive pattern across two extensions per ADR-0001's "two callers" rule.

Vertical slice: primitive + commit migration + existing commit tests passing + new unit tests for the primitive. Zero behavior change in the commit tool surface.

## Execution mode
AFK — scope is fully specified by the epic's architecture and durable decisions.

## Scope
**In the primitive (`src/shell/`):**
- `parseBash(command: string): Promise<ShellAst>` — tree-sitter-bash parse with lazy parser init, wasm paths via `createRequire` / `require.resolve` relative to the new location.
- `ShellAst` type alias (re-exports `web-tree-sitter` `Tree`).
- Colocated files: `index.ts`, `types.ts`, `index.test.ts`.

**Stays in commit extension:**
- `findGitCommits` (commit-specific AST walker).
- `GitCommitHit` type (commit-specific shape).
- Re-homed to `src/extensions/commit/shell.ts` (flat file, not a subdirectory) or merged into `guard.ts` — pick whichever keeps file count smallest per the anti-pattern "NO over-decomposed extension layout".

**Commit migration:**
- Commit extension imports `parseBash` and `ShellAst` from `@/shell/`.
- Commit extension uses `workspaceState` for any local persistence it currently does ad-hoc (audit: none today means migration reduces to the shell lift — note this in Outcome).

## Behaviors
Each becomes exactly one RED-GREEN-REFACTOR cycle.

1. `parseBash('echo hi')` returns a `ShellAst` with a non-null root node
2. `parseBash` tolerates an empty string without throwing
3. `parseBash` throws a clear error when tree-sitter returns `null` (defensive — document in a code comment)
4. Successive `parseBash` calls reuse the same parser instance (lazy-init caching observable via a single load)
5. Commit guard continues to detect `git commit` invocations after migration (existing guard tests pass unchanged)
6. Commit guard continues to detect `git commit --amend` after migration
7. Commit guard continues to detect `git-commit` hyphen alias after migration
8. `findGitCommits` lives in commit extension and imports only `ShellAst` from `src/shell/`

## Migration checklist
- [ ] Create `src/shell/{index.ts,types.ts,index.test.ts}` with `parseBash` + `ShellAst`.
- [ ] Move wasm path resolution so the new relative path (`../../vendor/tree-sitter-bash.wasm`) resolves correctly from `src/shell/`.
- [ ] Move `findGitCommits` + `GitCommitHit` to `src/extensions/commit/shell.ts` (single file) or inline into `guard.ts`.
- [ ] Delete `src/extensions/commit/shell/` directory.
- [ ] Update commit imports (`guard.ts`, any callers) to consume `src/shell/` for parsing and local module for `findGitCommits`.
- [ ] Ensure path aliases (`@/shell`) are wired in `tsconfig.json` / import-map if the project uses them; otherwise relative imports.
- [ ] Audit commit extension for ad-hoc state persistence that should move to `workspaceState` (expected: none in v1; record finding in Outcome).

## References
- `src/extensions/commit/shell/index.ts:1-117` — current primitive-shaped code
- `src/extensions/commit/shell/types.ts` — split: `ShellAst` to primitive, `GitCommitHit` stays in commit
- `src/workspaceState/index.ts` — API shape to mirror for any new namespacing
- `src/config/` — recent primitive; consistent file layout reference
- ADR-0001 (primitives require two callers) — commit + TDD-guard-future satisfies this
- ADR-0002 (file naming) — `index.ts`, `index.test.ts` colocation

## Out of scope
- TDD guard implementation (later task; it becomes the second caller at runtime, but the primitive is justified now by the architecture decision).
- ADR-0005 (guard composition) — ships with TDD guard task.
- Any new bash-AST utilities (write detection, redirection parsing) — TDD guard task adds these as commit-independent helpers in `src/shell/` or the tdd extension.
- Changes to commit tool user-facing behavior.

## Success criteria
- [ ] `src/shell/` exists with `parseBash` + `ShellAst` and unit tests for behaviors 1-4
- [ ] `src/extensions/commit/shell/` removed; `findGitCommits` re-homed to commit extension
- [ ] All existing commit tests (`guard.test.ts`, `tool.test.ts`) pass unchanged
- [ ] `pnpm check` passes (typecheck, oxlint, ast-grep, oxfmt, prettier, vitest)
- [ ] No import cycles; commit depends on `src/shell/`, not the reverse
- [ ] ast-grep rule `types-before-runtime-code` passes on new files

Dependencies:
notes: Lifted parseBash + ShellAst to src/shell/. Moved findGitCommits + GitCommitHit to flat src/extensions/commit/shell.ts. Removed old dir. Guard updated. New tests cover behaviors 1-4. Audit: commit extension has no ad-hoc state persistence — workspaceState migration is a no-op today. pnpm check passes (92 tests).

title: Task 1: commit tool — typed, validating, executing against a temp git repo
description:
## Goal

Deliver a working, test-driven `commit` tool end-to-end: typed schema registered with Pi, validation for subject and file paths, real git staging + commit via `pi.exec` against a temp repo, structured success and failure shapes. This is the load-bearing primitive; the guard, slash command, and SKILL.md are built on top in later tasks.

**Execution mode:** HITL. Strict TDD is new, the test infrastructure for a temp-git-repo integration is new, and the user explicitly wants every behavior walked through a full RED-GREEN-REFACTOR cycle with human confirmation between cycles.

## Behaviors

Each behavior is exactly one RED-GREEN-REFACTOR cycle. Run `cape:test-driven-development` per behavior. Do not batch. Do not pre-write implementation ahead of its test. For each cycle: write the failing test, run it and prove it fails with the expected message, write the minimum code to pass, run it and prove it passes, consider refactor (explicitly choose refactor or "no refactor needed"), then move to the next behavior. No single commit should contain more than one behavior's RED → GREEN transition.

1. **`validateSubject` throws a validation error naming the subject when the input does not match the conventional-commit regex.** Drives: `tool.ts` file creation, `types.ts` with `CommitInput` type, minimal `validateSubject(subject: string)` helper throwing unconditionally in GREEN. Test a subject like `"Add stuff."`.

2. **`validateSubject` returns without throwing when the subject matches the conventional-commit regex.** Drives: the actual regex `^(feat|fix|chore|refactor|docs|test|style|perf|build|ci|revert)(\([a-z0-9-]+\))?!?: .+` extracted as a locked constant in `tool.ts`. Pair with behavior 1 to avoid a tautology (rejects-all). Test `"feat: add thing"`, `"fix(scope): do it"`, `"chore!: breaking"`.

3. **`validatePaths` throws an error naming the offending path when any file matches the sensitive-path denylist.** Drives: `validatePaths(files: string[])` helper, locked denylist constant covering `.env`, `.env.*`, `*credentials*`, `*secret*`, `*.pem`, `*.key`, `id_rsa`, `id_rsa.*`, `id_ed25519`, `id_ed25519.*`, `.ssh/*`. Test with `[".env"]`, `["db/credentials.json"]`, `["keys/id_rsa"]`, `[".ssh/config"]`. The thrown error names which path matched.

4. **`validatePaths` returns without throwing when all files are outside the denylist.** Drives: glob matching logic (not just `includes`). Test with `["src/foo.ts", "README.md", "docs/env.md"]` — note `docs/env.md` must NOT match because it is not `.env` nor `.env.*`. Pair with behavior 3 to avoid a rejects-all tautology.

5. **The `commit` tool creates exactly one commit on a temp git repo when given valid inputs, and `details.sha` matches `git rev-parse HEAD`.** Drives: the full `execute()` path — `pi.exec("git", ["add", ...files])`, `pi.exec("git", ["commit", "-m", subject])`, capture of stdout for sha parsing, return of the success shape `{ content: [{ type: "text", text: "<sha> <subject>" }], details: { sha, files, subject } }`. Drives: `index.ts` with `pi.registerTool(commitTool)`. Test: create a temp directory, `git init`, write one tracked file, call the tool, assert `git log --oneline | wc -l` is 1, the subject appears in `git log -1`, and the returned sha matches `git rev-parse HEAD`.

6. **When `body` is provided, the committed message contains `<subject>\n\n<body>`.** Drives: message assembly logic in `execute()`. Test: call the tool with `subject: "feat: add"`, `body: "Longer explanation here."`; assert `git log -1 --format=%B` on the temp repo matches exactly `feat: add\n\nLonger explanation here.\n`.

7. **When `git commit` exits non-zero (pre-commit hook fails), the tool throws an error whose detail contains `hookFailed: true`, `stderr`, and `stdout`.** Drives: `HookFailedError` class (or equivalent structured throw), exit-code inspection after `pi.exec`. Test: create a temp repo whose `.git/hooks/pre-commit` is a script that writes to stderr and exits 1, stage a file, call the tool, assert the thrown error has `error.detail?.hookFailed === true`, `error.detail.stderr` non-empty, `error.detail.stdout` defined, AND assert `git log` on the repo still shows zero commits (the failed attempt did not create one).

## References

Similar patterns to look at while implementing:

- `node_modules/@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent/docs/extensions.md:1030-1092` — `pi.registerTool` with typebox schema, `execute()` signature, `pi.exec` usage, error semantics (throw to set `isError`).
- `node_modules/@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent/examples/extensions/auto-commit-on-exit.ts:11-49` — real-world example of `pi.exec("git", [...])` for staging and committing, including exit-code inspection.
- `node_modules/@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent/dist/core/extensions/loader.js:189` — confirms `pi.exec` is a direct `execCommand` delegation (bypasses `tool_call` event).
- `docs/adr/0002-file-naming-conventions.md` — camelCase `.ts`, colocated `*.test.ts`, no barrel `index.ts`.
- `docs/adr/0003-externally-observable-identifiers.md` — wire name `commit` and the success/failure return shapes are contracts; do not rename as a side effect of a refactor.
- `docs/adr/0004-skill-authoring-style.md` — relevant later; this task writes no SKILL.md.
- User rules `testing-typescript.md` and `testing-go.md` — prefer real infrastructure; tests use a real temp git repo, never mock `pi.exec`.
- `vitest` is the test runner (`package.json:22`); `pnpm test` runs it.

## Success criteria

- [ ] `src/extensions/commit/tool.ts` exists with the `commit` tool definition and typebox schema `{ files: Array<string>, subject: string, body?: string }`.
- [ ] `src/extensions/commit/types.ts` exists with `CommitInput`, `CommitSuccess`, `CommitHookFailure` types. No value code.
- [ ] `src/extensions/commit/index.ts` exists, imports from `tool.ts`, exports a default Pi wiring function that calls `pi.registerTool(commitTool)`. No `pi.registerCommand` or `pi.on` calls in this task (those are later tasks).
- [ ] `src/extensions/index.ts` imports and installs the commit extension.
- [ ] `src/extensions/commit/tool.test.ts` exists with one test per behavior above, plus shared temp-repo setup/teardown helpers.
- [ ] Every test uses a real temp git repository; `pi.exec` is never mocked; `validateSubject` / `validatePaths` are exercised through their real call sites (the tool's `execute`) where the behavior is end-to-end, or directly as unit tests for pure validation behaviors.
- [ ] After the task is complete, `pi.getAllTools()` (or equivalent access after extension load) contains an entry with `name: "commit"`.
- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` passes.
- [ ] `pnpm lint` passes.
- [ ] `pnpm lint:rules` passes.
- [ ] `pnpm format:check` passes.
- [ ] `pnpm test` passes with all new tests green.
- [ ] Git history for this task shows one RED-GREEN(-REFACTOR) cycle per behavior. Evidence: each behavior has at least one commit whose diff contains a failing test (RED) followed by a commit (or same commit with demonstrated prior failure) containing the implementation (GREEN). No behavior's implementation lands before its test.
- [ ] No file outside `src/extensions/commit/`, `src/extensions/index.ts`, and `src/extensions/commit/tool.test.ts` is modified except `package.json` (only if a new devDep is genuinely required for this task, which it should not be — `@sinclair/typebox` and `vitest` should already be available transitively).
- [ ] `src/extensions/commit/tool.ts` source contains no tokens for bulk-add, skip-hooks, or rewrite-last-commit variants.
- [ ] No `skills/commit/SKILL.md`, `src/extensions/commit/guard.ts`, or `src/extensions/commit/shell/` exist after this task (those are later tasks; this slice intentionally stops at the tool).
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks pass on the final commit of the task.

title: Task 2: shell parser — tree-sitter-bash wasm, parseBash, findGitCommits
description:
## Goal

Deliver an extension-local shell parser at `src/extensions/commit/shell/` that loads `tree-sitter-bash` via `web-tree-sitter`, parses bash command strings into ASTs, and walks them to detect git-commit invocations. This is the detection engine the guard (Task 3) will consume.

**Execution mode:** HITL. First use of tree-sitter in the project, wasm loading is new, and strict TDD applies.

## Behaviors

Each behavior is one RED-GREEN-REFACTOR cycle.

1. **`parseBash` returns a tree-sitter AST for a simple command string.** Drives: `shell/index.ts` creation, lazy wasm parser singleton, `parseBash(command: string)` function. Drives: `vendor/tree-sitter-bash.wasm` committed, `web-tree-sitter` added to devDependencies. Test: parse `"echo hello"`, assert root node type is `"program"`, child count > 0.

2. **`findGitCommits` returns an empty array for non-commit git commands.** Drives: `shell/types.ts` with `GitCommitHit` type, `findGitCommits` function. Test with negative corpus: `"git status"`, `"git diff HEAD"`, `"git log --oneline"`, `"git add src/foo.ts"`, `"echo 'git commit -m foo'"` (literal string mention). All return `[]`.

3. **`findGitCommits` detects a plain `git commit` invocation.** Test: `"git commit -m 'feat: add'"` returns one hit. Assert hit includes the node range or command text.

4. **`findGitCommits` detects `git commit` with the rewrite-last-commit flag variant.** Test: `"git commit --amend -m 'fix: typo'"` returns one hit with an `amend: true` flag (or equivalent marker distinguishing it from plain commit).

5. **`findGitCommits` detects `git commit` inside `&&`-chains, pipelines, command substitutions, and subshells.** Test corpus: `"git add . && git commit -m 'feat: x'"`, `"echo foo | git commit --allow-empty-message"`, `"echo $(git commit -m 'x')"`, `"(git commit -m 'x')"`. Each returns at least one hit.

6. **`findGitCommits` detects hyphen-dispatched `git-commit`.** Test: `"git-commit -m 'feat: add'"` returns one hit.

7. **`findGitCommits` returns an empty array for pipe-to-grep patterns that mention commit as a string argument.** Test: `"git log | grep commit"`, `"git log --format='%s' | grep 'commit'"`. Both return `[]`. This pairs with behavior 3 to prevent over-matching.

## References

- `node_modules/@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent/docs/extensions.md` — npm dependency support under jiti.
- [tree-sitter/tree-sitter-bash](https://github.com/tree-sitter/tree-sitter-bash) — official grammar, wasm target.
- `web-tree-sitter` npm package — wasm consumer for tree-sitter grammars.
- `~/.claude/projects/-home-sqve-code-personal-tau/memory/feedback_shell_parsing.md` — user feedback: prefer tree-sitter over regex/handwritten parsers.
- `docs/adr/0001-application-structure.md` — `vendor/` provision for third-party binary assets.
- `docs/adr/0002-file-naming-conventions.md` — camelCase `.ts`, colocated tests.

## Success criteria

- [ ] `src/extensions/commit/shell/index.ts` exists with `parseBash` and `findGitCommits` exports.
- [ ] `src/extensions/commit/shell/types.ts` exists with `GitCommitHit` type. No value code.
- [ ] `src/extensions/commit/shell/index.test.ts` exists with one test per behavior.
- [ ] `vendor/tree-sitter-bash.wasm` committed with an adjacent version note file.
- [ ] `package.json` declares `web-tree-sitter` in devDependencies. `pnpm install` succeeds.
- [ ] Parser loads lazily from a shared singleton promise (not re-initialized per call).
- [ ] `findGitCommits` walks `command_name` nodes, recurses into `command_substitution` and `subshell` children.
- [ ] Positive corpus (behaviors 3-6) all detected. Negative corpus (behaviors 2, 7) all clean.
- [ ] No regex-based command matching — all detection via tree-sitter AST walking.
- [ ] Tests use real wasm parser, never mocked.
- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` passes.
- [ ] `pnpm lint` passes.
- [ ] `pnpm lint:rules` passes.
- [ ] `pnpm format:check` passes.
- [ ] `pnpm test` passes with all new tests green.
- [ ] No files outside `src/extensions/commit/shell/`, `vendor/`, and `package.json`/`pnpm-lock.yaml` modified.
- [ ] No `guard.ts` or `guard.test.ts` in this task (that is Task 3).
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks pass on the final commit.

title: Task 3: guard — tool_call handler blocking LLM-initiated bash git-commit
description:
## Goal

Deliver a `tool_call` event handler at `src/extensions/commit/guard.ts` that intercepts LLM-initiated bash calls, delegates to the shell parser from Task 2, and blocks git-commit invocations with a reason referencing the `commit` tool. Also includes the literal-substring secondary check for `sh -c` / `bash -c` eval bypasses. Wire the guard into `src/extensions/commit/index.ts` via `pi.on("tool_call", ...)`.

**Execution mode:** HITL. Guard correctness is critical — false positives break the workflow, false negatives defeat the purpose.

## Behaviors

Each behavior is one RED-GREEN-REFACTOR cycle.

1. **Guard returns undefined (no block) for non-bash tool calls.** Drives: `guard.ts` creation, handler function narrowing with `isToolCallEventType("bash", event)`. Test: pass a tool_call event with `toolName: "read"` — handler returns undefined.

2. **Guard returns undefined for bash commands that are not git-commit invocations.** Test with negative corpus from Task 2: `"git status"`, `"git diff HEAD"`, `"git log --oneline"`, `"git add src/foo.ts"`. All return undefined.

3. **Guard returns `{ block: true, reason }` for a plain `git commit` bash command.** Test: event with `input.command: "git commit -m 'feat: add'"`. Assert `block: true` and reason string references the `commit` tool.

4. **Guard blocks the full positive corpus.** Test: `"git commit --amend"`, `"git add . && git commit -m 'x'"`, `"echo $(git commit -m 'x')"`, `"(git commit -m 'x')"`, `"git-commit -m 'feat: add'"`. All return `{ block: true }`.

5. **Guard blocks `sh -c` and `bash -c` containing commit invocations via literal-substring check.** Test: `"sh -c 'git commit -m x'"`, `"bash -c 'git commit -m x'"`. Both return `{ block: true }`.

6. **Guard does NOT block `sh -c` or `bash -c` without commit invocations.** Test: `"sh -c 'git status'"`, `"bash -c 'echo hello'"`. Both return undefined.

7. **Guard does NOT block pipe-to-grep patterns mentioning commit.** Test: `"git log | grep commit"`. Returns undefined.

8. **Guard is registered in `index.ts` and does not fire on the tool's own git invocations.** Drives: update `src/extensions/commit/index.ts` to call `pi.on("tool_call", guardHandler)`. Test: end-to-end — run the commit tool against a temp repo (reuse Task 1 infrastructure), verify no spurious block events.

## References

- `src/extensions/commit/shell/index.ts` — `parseBash`, `findGitCommits` from Task 2.
- `node_modules/@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent/docs/extensions.md` — `pi.on("tool_call", ...)`, `isToolCallEventType`.
- `dist/core/agent-session.js:166-186` — `beforeToolCall` fires only for LLM-dispatched tool calls.
- Epic design: literal-substring secondary check is a fixed rule, not a parser extension.

## Success criteria

- [ ] `src/extensions/commit/guard.ts` exists with the guard handler export.
- [ ] `src/extensions/commit/guard.test.ts` exists with one test per behavior.
- [ ] `src/extensions/commit/index.ts` updated to register guard via `pi.on("tool_call", guardHandler)`.
- [ ] Guard blocks positive corpus: plain commit, rewrite-last-commit variant, `&&`-chained, piped, command-substitution, subshell, hyphen-dispatched, `sh -c`/`bash -c` eval variants.
- [ ] Guard does NOT block negative corpus: status, diff, log, `git add <path>`, literal-string mentions, pipe-to-grep.
- [ ] Guard does NOT fire on the tool's own git invocations (end-to-end test).
- [ ] Reason message references the `commit` tool by wire name.
- [ ] No regex-based command detection in the guard — all AST-based via shell parser (except the literal-substring `sh -c`/`bash -c` secondary check).
- [ ] Tests use real wasm parser, never mocked.
- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` passes.
- [ ] `pnpm lint` passes.
- [ ] `pnpm lint:rules` passes.
- [ ] `pnpm format:check` passes.
- [ ] `pnpm test` passes with all tests green.
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks pass on the final commit.

title: Task 4: /commit slash command — thin entrypoint delegating to skill
description:
## Goal

Register a `/commit` slash command via `pi.registerCommand` that delegates to the commit skill via `pi.sendUserMessage`. The handler picks `deliverAs: "followUp"` when idle, `deliverAs: "steer"` while streaming. This is a thin entrypoint — no feature logic, no argument parsing beyond forwarding.

**Execution mode:** HITL. First slash command in tau; need to verify Pi's command registration and `sendUserMessage` behavior.

## Behaviors

Each behavior is one RED-GREEN-REFACTOR cycle.

1. **`/commit` is registered as an extension command with `name: "commit"` and `source: "extension"`.** Drives: update `src/extensions/commit/index.ts` to call `pi.registerCommand("commit", { ... })`. Test: load the extension, verify `pi.getCommands()` contains an entry with `name: "commit"`.

2. **Handler calls `pi.sendUserMessage` with `deliverAs: "followUp"` when idle.** Test: simulate `ctx.isIdle() === true`, verify `sendUserMessage` was called with the skill prefix and `{ deliverAs: "followUp" }`.

3. **Handler calls `pi.sendUserMessage` with `deliverAs: "steer"` while streaming.** Test: simulate `ctx.isIdle() === false`, verify `sendUserMessage` was called with `{ deliverAs: "steer" }`.

4. **Handler forwards trailing arguments to the skill message.** Test: invoke with args `"--scope auth"`, verify `sendUserMessage` receives `"/skill:commit --scope auth"` (or equivalent concatenation).

5. **Handler sends `/skill:commit` with no trailing space when invoked with no arguments.** Test: invoke with empty args, verify message is exactly `"/skill:commit"`.

## References

- `node_modules/@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent/docs/extensions.md` — `pi.registerCommand`, `pi.sendUserMessage`, `ctx.isIdle()`, `deliverAs` options.
- Epic design: command is primary entrypoint; skill expansion fires on re-entry via `/skill:commit` prefix.
- `dist/core/agent-session.js:812-814` — `_expandSkillCommand` only fires on `/skill:` prefix.

## Success criteria

- [ ] `src/extensions/commit/index.ts` calls `pi.registerCommand("commit", { ... })`.
- [ ] `/commit` appears in `pi.getCommands()` with `name: "commit"`, `source: "extension"`.
- [ ] Idle invocation triggers `sendUserMessage` with `deliverAs: "followUp"`.
- [ ] Streaming invocation triggers `sendUserMessage` with `deliverAs: "steer"`.
- [ ] Arguments forwarded; no-arg invocation sends bare `/skill:commit`.
- [ ] No feature logic in the handler — delegation only.
- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` passes.
- [ ] `pnpm lint` passes.
- [ ] `pnpm lint:rules` passes.
- [ ] `pnpm format:check` passes.
- [ ] `pnpm test` passes with all tests green.
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks pass on the final commit.

title: Task 5: SKILL.md — commit skill procedure per ADR-0004
description:
## Goal

Write `skills/commit/SKILL.md` — the Markdown-first skill body that drives the LLM through the commit workflow. Conforms to ADR-0004: frontmatter with \`name: commit\`, body sections from the allowed set, no XML-like tags. The skill hard-depends on the \`commit\` tool with no fallback.

**Execution mode:** Standard. This is prose, not code — no TDD cycles, but the file must pass structural validation.

## Behaviors

1. **Frontmatter contains \`name: commit\` and a specific description.** The description explains what the skill does in one sentence.

2. **Body uses only ADR-0004 section names.** Allowed sections: \`When to use\`, \`Goal\`, \`Hard rules\`, \`Procedure\`, \`Checklist\`, \`See also\`. No XML-tagged sections.

3. **Procedure instructs the LLM to:** gather git state (\`git status --porcelain\`, \`git diff\`), identify logical groups of changes, propose each group with a subject and file list for user confirmation, call the \`commit\` tool per confirmed group, handle \`hookFailed\` errors by reading stderr and fixing the underlying issue, cap retries at 3 per group, loop until the working tree is clean or the user stops.

4. **Hard rules section includes:** use the \`commit\` tool (no bash \`git commit\`), never stage with \`-A\` or \`.\`, never pass \`--no-verify\`, never rewrite history (\`--amend\`), always get user confirmation before each commit, conventional-commit subject format.

5. **Skill references the \`commit\` tool by its exact wire name.** No fallback path documented. Missing tool = loud failure.

6. **Hook failure handling:** on \`hookFailed\`, read the \`stderr\` field, diagnose the failure, fix the issue (lint, format, test), re-stage, retry. Cap at 3 retries per group. After 3 failures, stop and report to the user.

## References

- \`docs/adr/0004-skill-authoring-style.md\` — section names, frontmatter shape, no XML tags.
- Epic design: retry cap at 3, evidence-driven hook failure handling, no auto-fix in the tool.
- \`package.json:29-31\` — \`pi.skills = ["./skills"]\` declares the skill directory.

## Success criteria

- [ ] \`skills/commit/SKILL.md\` exists.
- [ ] Frontmatter has \`name: commit\` and a \`description\` field.
- [ ] Body sections are drawn exclusively from ADR-0004 allowed names.
- [ ] No XML-like section tags (\`<section>\`, \`</section>\`, etc.).
- [ ] Procedure covers: git state gathering, logical grouping, user confirmation, tool invocation, hook-failure retry (capped at 3), loop-until-clean.
- [ ] Hard rules cover: use \`commit\` tool only, no \`-A\`/\`.\`, no \`--no-verify\`, no \`--amend\`, user confirmation required, conventional-commit format.
- [ ] Tool referenced by wire name \`commit\` — no fallback path.
- [ ] \`pnpm typecheck\` passes (no TS changes expected, but verify).
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks pass on the final commit.
close_reason: Implemented skills/commit/SKILL.md per ADR-0004 and verified pnpm typecheck.
